## [00:00 - 03:02]

Upsells is something a lot of people struggle with. And the reality is today to make an offer work on cold traffic, you need to have really dialed in upsells. That's not like a fake specific number on the first column. And it's at roughly two times the price. And I'll get into all this. ## [03:02 - 06:02]

But there's a couple of key things that made the difference. And I think this is one of them, just really thinking about what they want next after they purchase that thing and not guessing. Instead of trying to guide them, we just dug through a lot of responses and what the pain was and what the concern was if they actually did succeed getting the result that they were buying. It's not as big as this other thing that I'm about to get to. It's called the sunny side upsell sequence. That's what we call it internally. Anytime we would try for continuity or recurring, it would lower the AOV for that, for day zero, you know, and a lot of the affiliates we were working with just wanted the money now. Yeah, so that's a story for another time, but because of company dynamics, it's tough for us to really go for the higher ticket stuff and we're just now starting to. ## [39:26 - 41:39]

But then one day we decided to stack them based on the EPC. So this one, let's see, this is Call-A-Mae. Yeah, so this one is with the stack based on the EPC that it was earning us based on the reports that we pulled before. And actually, a lot of these are Sunny's offers, which is funny. But so the thing is, a lot of these offers don't have the same tone that our FESL has, which is, I mean, we go for authentic. There's always a little bit of hype in there, but we're trying to make it feel authentic. And then the affiliate offers that we promote right afterwards have a much different feel. They're more hypey. And so one day we decided to just put an offer that we just swapped these two. And so this second one here has a talking head, like a real person speaking to the camera. And it's just a much different feel. It feels more authentic. And but it doesn't normally convert as well. And so it was interesting just by switching these two things. If you look at the numbers here, so it didn't, no, no, sorry, that's the first one. These are different times. I think I pulled the wrong one. But in the end, basically, it made a huge difference in everything. And my theory behind it is just that you want to keep the same tone of your offer with the promos as long as you can, because that'll keep the trust and engagement up. And if you just jump into something that's dramatically different right after bringing them through this emotional conversion, it's probably not going to feel right. So we've done a lot of things along those lines. We've created more and more of our own offers and a bundle of all of our stuff to sell them all of that first. I know this is obvious, but it's just stuff that we've recently tested. But yeah, just looking at your offer and trying to find the most aligned and feel and tone, putting those first, for us at least, we've done some tests around that and it made a big difference. So that's all I got unless there's any questions. Thank you.
